evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $292.6 million for the six months ended November 30, 2014 , an increase of $70.3 million compared to the same period last fiscal year.
Capital expenditures were $113.0 million and $76.8 million for the six months ended November 30, 2014 and November 30, 2013 , respectively.

During the first six months of fiscal 2014, under the October 18, 2011 program, we purchased 3.2 million shares of Cintas common stock for a total purchase price of $157.7 million. [...] During the six months ended November 30, 2014, under the July 30, 2013 program, we purchased 0.8 million shares of Cintas common stock for a total purchase price of $49.7 million.
Cintas is subject to legal proceedings and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business [...] management believes the aggregate liability [...] will not have a material adverse effect.

factors.summary: Demand appears to be growing, particularly through new business sales and customer penetration.

evidence.text: Rental Uniforms and Ancillary Products operating segment revenue increased 7.6% for the three months ended November 30, 2014 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $804.3 million to $865.4 million. Revenue increased organically by 8.1%. Growth was driven by many factors including new business sold by sales representatives and penetration of additional products and services into existing customers.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, as evidenced by increased revenue and cost efficiency.

evidence.text: The operating segment’s gross margin was $387.5 million, or 44.8% of revenue. The gross margin was 190 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s second quarter gross margin of 42.9%. The increase was driven by many factors including increased revenue from existing customers and continuously improving the efficiency of processes.

factors.summary: Gross margins are consistently expanding across the periods and segments discussed, contributing to improved profitability. The increases are driven by factors such as higher revenue from existing customers and process efficiency improvements.

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 43.8% for the six months ended November 30, 2014, which is a 30 basis point increase compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 43.5% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2014 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2013

evidence.text: The operating segment’s gross margin was $387.5 million, or 44.8% of revenue. The gross margin was 190 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s second quarter gross margin of 42.9%. The increase was driven by many factors including increased revenue from existing customers and continuously improving the efficiency of processes.

factors.summary: Cost actions have had a mixed impact on margins and expenses. While cost actions have contributed to margin improvements and savings, there has been an increase in labor and other employee-related expenses.

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses as a percent of revenue improved to 34.4% from 34.7% in the six months ended November 30, 2013, as revenue growth outpaced the increase in expenses.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2014 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2013

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses decreased $14.6 million, or 4.6%, for the three months ended November 30, 2014, compared to the three months ended November 30, 2013. The majority of the decrease was due to the deconsolidation of the shredding business. Selling and administrative expenses increased $34.7 million due primarily to labor and other employee-partner related expenses.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is improving and accelerating, particularly in the Rental Uniforms and Ancillary Products segment, which saw a 7.6% increase in revenue and an 8.1% organic growth rate. The growth is attributed to new business sales and increased product penetration among existing customers.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 11.1% as a result of increased sales volume.
